steps.title: Identify the competing 'first' claims
steps.content: Text 1 claims Carracci (c. 1585) was “the first artist to depict themself painting” in Bologna. Text 2 contradicts this, calling Fontana's 1577 painting “the earliest example of such a work by an artist from Bologna”—eight years earlier.

steps.title: Evaluate the response to the 'first' claim
steps.content: Text 2 directly undermines Carracci's status as “first” by presenting an earlier work (Fontana's 1577 vs. Carracci's c. 1585) that also references the artistic craft through the background easel. The other choices introduce unsupported elements—widespread respect, artistic intent, or inspiration. That matches choice D — D is correct.
